items.title: Interactive scatter EDA with hover metadata
items.scenario: A tabular ML team loads a training DataFrame and calls `px.scatter(df, x='age', y='income', color='churn', hover_data=['customer_id', 'region'])`. Reviewers zoom into a dense cluster and hover individual points to read IDs before flagging labeling errors.
items.explanation: Plotly Express maps columns to encodings and packs extra fields into tooltips—interactive inspection Matplotlib scatter cannot match without custom annotation code.

items.title: Train vs validation loss with unified epoch hover
items.scenario: After each epoch, lists `train_loss` and `val_loss` feed a Graph Objects figure with two `go.Scatter` traces and `hovermode='x unified'`. The ML engineer hovers at epoch 42 and sees both losses in one tooltip, spotting val divergence early.
items.explanation: Unified hover on shared x-axes is a standard monitoring pattern for training curves; Graph Objects gives explicit trace and layout control.

items.title: 3D embedding preview for a stakeholder demo
items.scenario: PCA or UMAP coordinates in columns `x`, `y`, `z` with `label` and `text` metadata become `px.scatter_3d(df, x='x', y='y', z='z', color='label', hover_name='text')`. A product manager rotates the view in the browser to explore cluster separation before a deeper embedding review.
items.explanation: Plotly supplies rotatable 3D interaction; interpretation guardrails and projection semantics belong to the embedding visualization topic.

items.title: HTML dashboard snippet for experiment comparison
items.scenario: A researcher saves `fig.write_html('run_17_metrics.html')` after building a two-row subplot of loss and F1 vs epoch, then shares the file in Slack. Teammates open it locally—no Jupyter server—and toggle the validation trace off to compare runs visually.
items.explanation: Self-contained HTML export is Plotly's lightweight sharing path for ML experiment review before investing in a full Dash deployment.
